name: discreteBodeData
doc: Compute Bode data for a discrete transfer function.

name: findEquilibrium
doc: Newton-Raphson iteration to find an equilibrium x* such that f(x*)=0, starting from initial guess x0.

name: classifyEquilibrium
doc: Classify an equilibrium point via Jacobian eigenvalues.   type: 'stable-node'|'unstable-node'|'saddle'|'stable-spiral'|'unstable-spiral'|'center'|'unknown'   stable: true if all eigenvalues have Re < 0   stiffness: max|Re(λ)| / min|Re(λ)| (stiffness ratio)

name: scanEquilibria
doc: Scan for multiple equilibria by running Newton-Raphson from a grid of starting points.   Unique equilibria found (deduplicated within tolerance)

name: bodeData
doc: Compute Bode plot data.

name: nyquistData
doc: Compute Nyquist plot data. Returns real and imaginary parts for positive and negative frequencies.

name: autoFreqRange
doc: Auto-detect frequency range based on poles and zeros.

name: nicholsData
doc: Compute Nichols chart data (open-loop phase vs. magnitude).

name: nyquistEncirclements
doc: Count Nyquist encirclements of the -1+j0 point. Positive = clockwise encirclement (indicates instability for stable open-loop). Uses winding number algorithm.

name: phasePortrait
doc: Build a phase portrait for the autonomous 2D system  ẋ = f(x).   trajectories: array of [t, x1, x2] sequences

name: linearVelocityField
doc: Build a 2D state-space velocity field from a linear system:  ẋ = A x + B u(t),  u≡0.

name: describingFunction
doc: Describing function N(A) of common nonlinearities, returning the equivalent complex gain as a function of input amplitude A. Useful for predicting limit cycles via 1 + N(A)·G(jω) = 0. type: 'saturation' | 'relay' | 'deadzone'

name: rootLocusData
doc: Compute root locus data. For characteristic equation: den(s) + K·num(s) = 0

name: rootLocusAsymptotes
doc: Compute asymptotes for root locus.

name: sortRootLocusBranches
doc: Sort each step's root array so that branch index is continuous across K. Greedy nearest-neighbor matching between consecutive steps.

name: rootLocusBreakPoints
doc: Find breakaway / break-in points on the real axis. Solves num'(s)·den(s) − num(s)·den'(s) = 0 and keeps real candidates with K > 0.

name: rootLocusJwCrossings
doc: Find K values where the locus crosses the imaginary axis (jω crossings). Sweeps K logarithmically, detects sign change in Re(root) on any branch, then refines each crossing by bisection on the parametric characteristic poly.

name: simulatePIDAntiWindup
doc: Simulate PID controller + plant in open-loop feedback with actuator saturation and back-calculation anti-windup. Anti-windup law:  dxi/dt = e + (u_sat - u_unsat) / Tt When u_unsat is within [uMin, uMax], the correction term is zero (normal integral). When saturated, the correction pulls the integrat

name: identifyRLS
doc: Create a stateful RLS estimator for a linear regression model:   y(t) = φ(t)ᵀ θ where θ ∈ ℝⁿ are unknown parameters and φ(t) is the regressor vector. Exponential forgetting: older data is down-weighted by λᵏ (0 < λ ≤ 1). λ = 1 → standard least squares (all history equally weighted). Update equations

name: designMRAC
doc: Design a MRAC controller for a first-order plant:   ẋ_p = a_p x_p + b_p u   (unknown a_p, b_p) tracking a reference model:   ẋ_m = a_m x_m + b_m r   (a_m < 0, b_m > 0 specified) MIT gradient rule adaptive laws:   k̇_r = −γ e x_m   (feed-forward gain from reference)   k̇_x = −γ e x_p   (state feedbac

name: selfTuningRegulator
doc: Self-Tuning Regulator: online RLS identification + pole-placement redesign. Plant model: A(q⁻¹) y(t) = B(q⁻¹) u(t) + e(t)   θ = [a1,...,na, b1,...,nb]ᵀ — estimated online via RLS. Controller redesign: at each step, use estimated {a_i, b_i} to compute a control law u(t) = −k_y · y(t) + k_r · r(t) tha

name: iterativeLearningControl
doc: P-type Iterative Learning Control for repetitive tasks. Given a desired output trajectory yd[0..T-1], ILC updates the feed-forward input sequence across trials to eliminate the repetitive error. Update law (P-type):   u_{k+1}(t) = u_k(t) + L · e_k(t+d) where e_k(t) = yd(t) − y_k(t), d is relative de

name: identifySRIVC
doc: Simplified Refined Instrumental Variable for Continuous-time (SRIVC). Estimates continuous-time ARX model parameters:   a(p) y(t) = b(p) u(t) + e(t) where p = d/dt, a(p) = pⁿ + a_{n-1}p^{n-1} + ... + a_0,               b(p) = b_{m} p^m + ... + b_0. Algorithm (Simplified / batch IV estimate):   1. Pr

name: notchFilter
doc: Notch filter transfer function. N(s) = (s² + 2·ζ_num·ω_n·s + ω_n²) / (s² + 2·ζ_den·ω_n·s + ω_n²) zetaNum < zetaDen gives attenuation at ω_n.

name: designLeadLagCompensator
doc: Design a Lead-Lag cascade compensator in one step. C(s) = Kc_lead·(τ₁s+1)/(ατ₁s+1) · (τ₂s+1)/(βτ₂s+1)   α<1, β>1 Lead part: provides phaseBoostDeg phase lift at crossoverFreq. Lag  part: provides improvementFactor steady-state gain boost (α_lag = improvementFactor).

name: padeCoefficients
doc: Pade approximant of e^{-sT}: a rational p(s)/q(s) of order n.   Numerator   p(s) = Σ_{k=0..n} a_k (Ts)^k   where a_k = (2n−k)! n! / [(2n)! k! (n−k)!] (−1)^k   Denominator q(s) = a_k with sign flipped to + (k always positive) The convention here uses high-order coefficient first (matches TransferFunc

name: padeApprox
doc: Returns a TransferFunction representing the n-th order Padé approximation of e^{-sT}.

name: applyDelay
doc: Compose a plant with a time delay. Returns G(s) · e^{-sT} approximated as G(s)·Pade(T, n).

name: delayPhase
doc: Phase contribution of a pure delay e^{-jωT}: φ(ω) = −ωT radians. Useful for analytical phase computation without Padé approximation.

name: delayMargin
doc: Delay margin (seconds): given phase margin PM (radians) at gain-crossover ω_gc, DM = PM / ω_gc. This is the additional delay the loop can tolerate before instability.

name: smithPredictor
doc: Smith Predictor compensator structure for plants with dead time. The Smith predictor wraps an existing controller C(s) so that the effective loop transfer L(s) = C(s) · G(s) (delay-free) rather than C(s) · G(s) · e^{-sT}.      ┌────── G_m(s)·(1−e^{−sT_m}) ──────┐      │

name: specsToTargetPoles
doc: Map (%OS, Ts) specifications to a 2nd-order dominant pole pair.   ζ  = -ln(OS/100) / √(π² + ln²(OS/100))   σ  = k / Ts        (k=4 for 2%, k=3 for 5%)   ωn = σ / ζ   ωd = ωn · √(1 - ζ²)   target poles s = -σ ± j ωd

name: designLeadForPM
doc: Design a Lead compensator that lifts plant phase margin to `targetPM`. Strategy: keep crossover frequency, add phase boost at that frequency.                   currentPM, targetPM, achievedPM, leadTf, params }}

name: autoTunePIDToSpec
doc: Auto-tune PID to meet a frequency-domain specification:   target phase margin PM_target at target crossover frequency ωc. Algorithm (loop-shaping):   1. Evaluate G(jωc) → magnitude |G|, phase φ_G   2. Required controller phase: φ_C = PM_target − 180° − φ_G   3. Solve for (Kp, Ki, Kd) from the two co

name: deadbeatGain
doc: Deadbeat state-feedback gain via Ackermann's formula.   K = [0 0 … 0 1] · Wc⁻¹ · α(A) where Wc = [B AB A²B … A^{n-1}B] and α(z) = zⁿ (all desired poles at 0). Therefore α(A) = Aⁿ. The resulting closed-loop A − BK has all eigenvalues at z = 0, settling in at most n samples.

name: computeMuUpperBound
doc: Compute the μ upper bound for a real matrix M via D-scaling:   μ̄(M) = min_{d>0} σ_max(D M D⁻¹)   where D = diag(d) Optimization is performed by gradient descent on log(d). For full-block unstructured Δ: optimal D = I, so μ̄ = σ_max(M).

name: computeMuBoundFreq
doc: Compute μ upper bound profile over a frequency grid.

name: dkIteration
doc: D-K iteration for robust H∞ synthesis. Starting from the plant state-space {A,B,C,D}, iterates:   K-step: H∞ synthesis on D-scaled plant   D-step: optimize D diagonal scaling at each frequency This simplified version uses frequency-independent (constant) D scaling and the loopShapingHinf controller

name: numericalJacobian
doc: Numerical Jacobian: J[i][j] = ∂f_i/∂x_j via central difference.

name: simulateEKF
doc: Run Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) on a sequence of measurements.   xhat[k] = posterior state estimate at time k   P[k] = posterior covariance at time k   innovations[k] = y[k] - h(x_prior[k]) (innovation vector)

name: simulateUKF
doc: Run Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) — sigma-point based, no Jacobians needed. Uses the standard scaled unscented transform with parameters (α=1e-3, β=2, κ=0).

name: runLinearEKF
doc: Convenience: run EKF (or UKF) on a linearized discrete-time SS model {Ad, Bd, Cd} + a step control sequence. Simulates the true states from the model with process noise, then runs EKF/UKF on the simulated measurements.

name: differentialEvolution
doc: Minimise fn(x) over x ∈ [lb, lb+range]^d using DE/rand/1/bin.

name: simulateEMPC
doc: Simulate Economic MPC via Differential Evolution open-loop optimisation.                                   Must return a finite scalar for any (x,u).   x:          number[][],   // closed-loop state trajectory (steps+1 × n)   u:          number[][],   // applied control sequence (steps × m)   stageC

name: movingHorizonEstimation
doc: Moving Horizon Estimator for linear discrete-time systems. Problem (horizon N):   min  Σ_{k=t-N}^{t} ‖y_k − C x_k‖²_R⁻¹ + ‖w_k‖²_Q⁻¹ + ‖x̄_{t-N} − x₀‖²_P₀⁻¹   s.t. x_{k+1} = A x_k + B u_k + w_k        xMin ≤ x_k ≤ xMax  (optional) Solved as a condensed least-squares QP (active-set for simplicity).

name: particleFilter
doc: Particle filter (Bootstrap / SIR) for nonlinear state estimation. State transition: x_{k+1} = f(x_k, u_k) + w_k,   w_k ~ N(0, Q) Observation:      y_k = h(x_k) + v_k,              v_k ~ N(0, R)

name: designFDD
doc: Design a model-based FDD system using residual generation. Residual: r(t) = y(t) − ŷ(t|t-1)  (innovation / output error) Fault detection: alarm when ‖r(t)‖ > threshold (based on Gaussian statistics). Fault diagnosis: compare residual pattern to fault signatures.   and `.reset()`.

name: reconfigurableFTC
doc: Reconfigurable fault-tolerant controller. Maintains a bank of controllers (one nominal + one per fault mode). After fault isolation by FDD, switches to the appropriate controller.

name: gaTunePID
doc: Run the GA. Returns the best PID parameters and the convergence history.

name: nsga2TunePID
doc: NSGA-II multi-objective PID tuner. Objectives: [overshoot%, settlingTime] by default (optionally iae/effort). Returns a Pareto front of non-dominated solutions sorted by first objective.

name: buildMixedSensitivityPlant
doc: Build generalized plant P from SISO plant G and weights W1, W2, W3. Static-gain weights (null SS) are handled via D-matrix only.

name: tfToSS
doc: Convert TF to controllable-canonical SS. Returns null for static gains.

name: gammaIteration
doc: Glover-Doyle H∞ synthesis via γ-iteration. Simplified assumptions for SISO mixed-sensitivity: - D22 = 0 (or small) - D11 handled via loop-shifting when non-zero Returns { controller, gamma, Xinf, Yinf, closedLoopPoles, ... }

name: synthesizeHinfRiccati
doc: Full Riccati-based H∞ mixed-sensitivity synthesis for SISO plants.

name: loopShapingHinf
doc: Loop Shaping H∞ synthesis via normalized coprime factor robust stabilization. (McFarlane & Glover, 1992) Procedure:   1. Form shaped plant Gs = W2 · G · W1  (default W1=W2=1)   2. Solve two CAREs for the normalized LCF of Gs:        X∞ CARE: As'X + XAs + Qs_x − X·Rs_x·X = 0        Y∞ CARE: As·Y + Y·

name: mixedSensitivityCost
doc: For each frequency, compute the maximum singular value of the stacked sensitivity vector [W1·S ; W2·KS ; W3·T]. In SISO this is simply sqrt(|W1 S|² + |W2 KS|² + |W3 T|²).

name: tunePIDForMixedSensitivity
doc: Nelder-Mead simplex search for PID parameters (Kp, Ki, Kd). Minimises the mixed-sensitivity H∞ cost subject to closed-loop stability.

name: synthesizeDynamicMixedSensitivity
doc: Fixed/full-order dynamic mixed-sensitivity H∞ synthesis. This solves the same weighted objective as mixedSensitivityCost, but searches over a stable proper dynamic controller K(s)=N(s)/D(s). When order is omitted, it uses plant.order, giving a full-order controller relative to the plant. The impleme

name: defaultMixedSensitivityWeights
doc: Convenience: build typical mixed-sensitivity weights.   W1 = (s/M + ω_B) / (s + ω_B·A_low)  — performance (low-freq integrator-like)   W3 = (s + ω_B/M)·A_high / (M·s + ω_B) — high-freq T constraint   W2 = constant (control effort)

name: analyzeLPV
doc: Check common quadratic stability of an LPV system. Solves: ∃P ≻ 0 s.t. Aᵢᵀ P + P Aᵢ ≺ 0 for all i in grid.

name: synthesizeLPV
doc: Design a common quadratic-stabilizing state-feedback gain K for an LPV system. Solves: ∃Q ≻ 0, L s.t.   Aᵢ Q + Q Aᵢᵀ − Bᵢ L − Lᵀ Bᵢᵀ ≺ 0   ∀i then K = L Q⁻¹.   A: n×n state matrix, B: n×m input matrix (same n,m across all points).

name: parseMIMOMatrices
doc: Parse MIMO state-space from textarea inputs.

name: dcGain
doc: Compute DC gain matrix G(0) = -C·A^{-1}·B + D

name: rgaSteady
doc: Compute RGA = G ⊗ (G^{-T}) for square m×m system at steady state.

name: staticDecoupler
doc: Static decoupler: W = G(0)^{-1} (yields G(0)·W = I). Returns m×m static matrix W. Caller can apply via applyDecoupler.

name: dynamicDecouplerAtFrequency
doc: Frequency-specific dynamic decoupler prototype: W(jωc) = G(jωc)^-1, verification = G(jωc)·W(jωc). This is not yet a polynomial matrix dynamic compensator. It is a selected- frequency inverse used to quantify whether dynamic decoupling is plausible around a chosen crossover frequency.

name: applyDecoupler
doc: Apply a static decoupler W (m×m) to a MIMO plant: B' = B·W, D' = D·W. Returns a new MIMOStateSpace with decoupled inputs.

name: rgaDiagnosis
doc: Diagnose pairing quality from RGA diagonal.

name: evalAtJw
doc: Evaluate complex matrix G(jω) = C·(jωI - A)^{-1}·B + D. Returns p×m grid of Complex numbers.

name: singularValues
doc: Singular values of complex matrix G via eigenvalues of G^H · G.

name: dynamicRGA
doc: Dynamic RGA: Λ(jω) = G(jω) .* (G(jω)^{-T})   where Λ_{ij} = G_{ij}(jω) · [G^{-1}(jω)]_{ji}

name: dynamicRGAMagnitude
doc: Like dynamicRGA but returns magnitudes |Λ_{ij}(jω)| as plain numbers.

name: dynamicRGADiagonal
doc: Returns only the diagonal RGA magnitudes [|Λ_{11}|, |Λ_{22}|, …] per frequency. Useful for monitoring coupling as a function of frequency.

name: characteristicLoci
doc: Characteristic loci: eigenvalues λ_i(G(jω)) across frequency. These are used in MIMO Nyquist-style design to see whether loop eigenvalues encircle the critical point after pairing / decoupling.

name: gershgorinBands
doc: Gershgorin bands for G(jω): center = G_ii(jω), radius = Σ_{j≠i}|G_ij(jω)|. A small radius/center ratio indicates weaker loop interaction around ω.

name: inverseNyquistArray
doc: Inverse Nyquist Array (INA): entries of G(jω)^-1 across frequency. Useful when designing diagonal controllers on inverse plant channels.

name: singularValueBode
doc: Compute σ_max(ω), σ_min(ω) across frequency grid.

name: minrealSS
doc: Compute the minimum realisation of a state-space system (A, B, C, D) by removing uncontrollable and unobservable states using SVD-based rank tests on the controllability and observability Gramians (or matrices). Algorithm:   1. Compute Wc = controllability Gramian (or Kalman matrix if A unstable)

name: balancedTruncation
doc: Balanced Truncation model order reduction for stable LTI systems. Algorithm (square-root method):   1. Solve Gramians: A·Wc + Wc·Aᵀ + B·Bᵀ = 0                      Aᵀ·Wo + Wo·A + Cᵀ·C  = 0   2. Cholesky: Wc = Lc·Lc', Wo = Lo·Lo'   3. SVD: Lc'·Lo = U·Σ·V'  →  Hankel singular values σᵢ = Σ[i]   4. Bal

name: hankelSingularValues
doc: Compute Hankel singular values of a stable LTI system (A, B, C, D). The Hankel singular values are the square roots of the eigenvalues of Wc · Wo, where Wc and Wo are the controllability and observability Gramians. Equivalently, they are the singular values of Lc^T · Lo.

name: hankelNorm
doc: Hankel norm of a stable LTI system — the largest Hankel singular value. ‖G‖_H = σ₁(Wc, Wo)

name: hankelNormApprox
doc: Hankel norm approximation of order k for a stable LTI system. Uses balanced truncation (P25-01) for the reduced state matrices, which for balanced realizations achieves the optimal Hankel norm error σ_{k+1} (Glover 1984 / AAK theorem).  The exact Hankel norm of the error system is computed numerical

name: finiteHorizonLqr
doc: Finite-horizon discrete LQR recursion for unconstrained MPC: P_N = Qf  (or P∞ from DARE when options.autoTerminalCost = true) K_k = (R + B'P_{k+1}B)^-1 B'P_{k+1}A P_k = Q + A'P_{k+1}A - A'P_{k+1}B K_k

name: solveSetpointSteadyState
doc: Compute the steady-state input u_ss required for the state x_ss = r. Steady-state condition: r = Ad·r + Bd·u_ss  →  (I−Ad)·r = Bd·u_ss Solution: u_ss = pinv(Bd) · (I−Ad) · r   (minimum-norm if underdetermined) Also returns the residual ‖Bd·u_ss − (I−Ad)·r‖∞ (= 0 when r is exactly reachable).

name: firstMpcActionTracking
doc: One step of receding-horizon setpoint-tracking MPC. The state error e_k = x_k − r and the input increment v_k = u_k − u_ss satisfy the SAME dynamics as (Ad, Bd), so standard MPC is run on e_0 and v returned. The actual control is u = v + u_ss. constraints: same shape as firstMpcActionConstrained ({

name: simulateMpcTracking
doc: Simulate setpoint-tracking MPC in receding-horizon fashion. reference: n×1 constant state target, or { r: n×1, steps: callable } for time-varying.

name: solveOutputSetpointSteadyState
doc: MIMO output-space setpoint steady-state solver. Finds x_ss, u_ss satisfying:   x_ss = A_d x_ss + B_d u_ss   y_ref = C x_ss + D u_ss Non-square plants use a regularized least-squares solution, so the residual explicitly reports whether the requested output setpoint is exactly reachable.

name: simulateMpcOutputTracking
doc: Receding-horizon MPC tracking for MIMO output references y_ref. reference may be a fixed p×1 matrix or a function (k) → p×1.

name: firstMpcActionConstrained
doc: Solve one constrained MPC step (receding-horizon: return only u_0). constraints:   { uMin: number|number[], uMax: number|number[] }   — per-step box bounds on u   { A: number[][], b: number[] }                     — general polyhedral: A·U_seq ≤ b   Both can be combined.  Per-input arrays or scalars

name: simulateConstrainedMpc
doc: Simulate constrained MPC in receding-horizon fashion. Returns same shape as simulateUnconstrainedMpc plus activeConstraintsLog.

name: firstMpcActionStateConstrained
doc: One step of MPC with both input box constraints and soft state constraints. uConstraints: { uMin, uMax }  (same as firstMpcActionConstrained) xConstraints: { xMin: number[], xMax: number[], penalty: number }   xMin/xMax are n-vectors of per-state bounds (use ±Infinity to skip a state).   penalty (ρ)

name: simulateStateConstrainedMpc
doc: Simulate MPC with input and soft state constraints in receding-horizon fashion.

name: buildDisturbanceModel
doc: Build augmented state-space model for offset-free tracking. Assumes output disturbances (d is added to y). x_aug = [x; d] d_{k+1} = d_k (random walk) y_k = C x_k + d_k

name: designDisturbanceObserver
doc: Design Kalman observer for the augmented offset-free tracking model.

name: simulateOffsetFreeMpc
doc: Simulate MPC with offset-free tracking (augmented observer in the loop).

name: checkMpcFeasibility
doc: Predict bounds of reachable state/output trajectory under input constraints, and check for hard conflicts with state/output constraints. This is a pessimistic box-reachability diagnostic tool.

name: simulateNMPC
doc: Simulate NMPC via successive linearization on a discrete nonlinear system.                                  x: number[n], u: number[m], returns number[n]   x:     number[][],  // state trajectory (steps+1 × n)   u:     number[][],  // control sequence (steps × m)   cost:  number[],    // stage cost

name: gainScheduledPID
doc: Build a gain-scheduled PID whose Kp, Ki, Kd are piecewise-linearly interpolated across breakpoints of a scheduling variable ρ.   - PID gains at each breakpoint (length must equal breakpoints.length)   getGains(rho: number): {Kp, Ki, Kd},   compute(e: number, dedt: number, intE: number, rho: number):

name: simulateGainScheduledPID
doc: Simulate a gain-scheduled PID controller on a 1st-order discrete plant. Plant model: y[k+1] = a·y[k] + b·u[k]  (discrete) Scheduling:  rho[k] = schedulingFn(k, y[k], u[k])

name: designSMC
doc: Design a sliding-mode controller for a SISO system in normal form:   ẋ₁ = x₂,  ẋ₂ = f(x) + g(x)·u Sliding surface:  σ(x) = c·e₁ + ė₁  (linear, c > 0) Control law:      u = u_eq + u_sw   u_eq = -(c·e₂ + f(x)) / g(x)          (equivalent control)   u_sw = -(η/ε)·sat(σ/ε)                 (switching wit

name: simulateSMC
doc: Simulate SMC on a 2nd-order continuous plant (Euler integration):   ẋ₁ = x₂   ẋ₂ = a·x₂ + b·u + d(t)    (d = disturbance)

name: feedbackLinearization
doc: Input-output feedback linearization for SISO affine systems:   ẋ = f(x) + g(x) u   y = h(x) If the relative degree r equals the state dimension n, exact state-space linearization is achieved. Otherwise, input-output linearization creates a linear relationship between the r-th derivative of y and a n

name: backstepping
doc: Backstepping controller for 2nd-order strict-feedback systems:   ẋ₁ = f₁(x₁) + x₂           (first subsystem)   ẋ₂ = f₂(x₁, x₂) + u        (second subsystem / actual input)   y  = x₁                      (output) Design:   Step 1: virtual control φ₁*(x₁) = −k₁ x₁ − f₁(x₁) + ẋ₁_ref           e₁ = x₂

name: controlBarrierFunction
doc: CLF-CBF safety filter via QP. Given a nominal control u_nom(x), find u* nearest to u_nom subject to:   CLF: V̇(x,u) ≤ −γ V(x)        (stability: V must decrease)   CBF: ḣ(x,u) ≥ −α h(x)         (safety: h ≥ 0 is safe set) Solved as:   min_{u, δ}  ‖u − u_nom‖² + λ_δ δ²   s.t.  LfV + LgV u + γ V ≤ δ

name: TwoDOFPIDController
doc: Two-degree-of-freedom PID controller. Reference: C_r(s) applied to setpoint r; C_y(s) applied to output y. C_r(s) = β·Kp + Ki/s + γ·Kd·N·s/(s+N) C_y(s) = Kp + Ki/s + Kd·N·s/(s+N) β: proportional reference weight (0-1, β=1 → 1-DOF PID) γ: derivative reference weight (0=no derivative kick on setpoint,

name: exportController
doc: Export a designed controller to executable code in C, Python, or MATLAB. Supported controller shapes:   PID   — { type:'pid', Kp, Ki, Kd, N?, Ts? }   TF    — { type:'tf',  num:number[], den:number[], Ts? }   SS    — { type:'ss',  A, B, C, D, Ts? }   SF    — { type:'sf',  K:number[], Ts? }     (state

name: generateDesignReport
doc: Generate an HTML design report from analysis results. The `design` object can contain any subset of:   title       — string   description — string   margins     — { phaseMargin, gainMargin, gainCrossover, phaseFreq }   poles       — number[][]  [[re,im], ...]   zeros       — number[][]   stepRespons

name: toPythonControl
doc: Serialize a ControlStudio object to a JSON structure compatible with python-control's to_dict / from_dict convention. Supported input shapes:   { type:'tf',  num, den, Ts? }   { type:'ss',  A, B, C, D, Ts? }   { type:'pid', Kp, Ki, Kd, N?, Ts? }  → converted to TF first python-control convention (St

name: fromPythonControl
doc: Deserialize a python-control JSON dict back to a ControlStudio descriptor.

name: designWizard
doc: Design Wizard — spec-driven controller architecture recommender. Takes a performance specification and returns:   - Recommended controller type & order   - Ordered design workflow steps   - Estimated design complexity   - Relevant ControlStudio API calls with parameters   recommendation: string,   c

name: additiveUncertaintyEnvelope
doc: Additive uncertainty: L_perturbed(jω) = L(jω) + ΔL(jω), where |ΔL(jω)| ≤ W_a(ω) at frequency ω. Returns worst-case |S|, |T|, |KS| over a disk perturbation of radius W_a sampled at `samples` angles. Use when the plant is poorly modeled in absolute terms (e.g. additive uncertainty Δ = G_real − G_model

name: diskMargin
doc: Disk Margin α: largest disk (gain k ∈ [1/(1+α), 1+α], phase φ ∈ [−θ_α, θ_α]) inside which closed-loop stability is preserved. Approximation via SISO sensitivity:   α ≈ 1 / max( ‖S‖∞ , ‖T‖∞ ) Returns { alpha, phaseDeg, gainDB } where:   phaseDeg = 2·asin(α/2) (degrees) — equivalent phase margin   gai

name: hInfNormUpperBound
doc: Estimate the H∞ norm using a coarse grid search only (no refinement). Faster but less accurate than hInfNorm.

name: hInfNorm
doc: Estimate the H∞ norm: max_ω σ_max(G(jω)). Two-pass strategy:   1. Coarse log-spaced grid (gridPoints points from omegaLo to omegaHi).   2. Golden-section search over [10^(log10(ω_peak)−2), 10^(log10(ω_peak)+2)]      for goldenIter iterations to refine the peak to ~0.1% relative accuracy.

name: structuredMuUpperBound
doc: Upper bound for the structured singular value μΔ(M). The browser implementation uses diagonal positive D-scaling:   μ(M) ≤ inf_D σ_max(D M D⁻¹) For SISO this is exact. For diagonal repeated scalar blocks this is the standard first-pass upper-bound used before a heavier DK-iteration backend.

name: structuredMuSynthesisSurrogate
doc: DK-style static gain search surrogate: choose scalar loop gain k that minimizes maxω μ(k·M(jω)). This is not industrial full DK iteration. It gives ControlStudio an explicit structured robust design loop with a reproducible objective and D-scales.

name: validateUncertaintyModel
doc: Validate and normalize a SISO transfer-function uncertainty model. Supported fields:   - gain: relative range, e.g. 0.2 => [0.8, 1.2] or [0.8, 1.2]   - numerator: per-coefficient relative ranges   - denominator: per-coefficient relative ranges; leading coefficient is kept normalized   - additive: ad

name: stabilityMargins
doc: Compute gain margin and phase margin from a transfer function.

name: stepInfo
doc: Compute step response performance metrics.

name: routhTable
doc: Compute the Routh-Hurwitz stability table for a given denominator polynomial.

name: analyzeStability
doc: Engineering stability summary for continuous or discrete SISO systems. Continuous systems are stable when every pole is in the open LHP. Discrete systems are stable when every pole is inside the unit circle.

name: solveCareHamiltonianSchur
doc: Continuous CARE by Hamiltonian stable invariant subspace. For n ≥ 5 the primary path is a real Schur decomposition (Francis QR) which is numerically more reliable than the eigenvector path for high-order systems. For n ≤ 4 the dependency-free eigenvector path is tried first (faster, same accuracy at

name: solveDAREHamiltonianSign
doc: Solve the Discrete Algebraic Riccati Equation (DARE) via:   1. Build the symplectic matrix M_d (2n×2n)   2. Cayley transform: C = (M_d − I)(M_d + I)⁻¹ maps |λ|<1 → Re<0   3. Matrix sign function (Newton iteration) on C to isolate stable subspace   4. Extract P = Y X⁻¹ from the stable invariant subsp

name: solveLqrMIMO
doc: MIMO LQR via Newton–Kleinman iteration. Accepts R as m×m matrix. Returns K (m×n), P (n×n), and convergence diagnostics.

name: discretizeZOH
doc: Discretize a continuous state-space model using Zero-Order Hold (ZOH). Uses the matrix exponential augmented method: Ad = e^(A*Ts), Bd via integral.

name: innovationStats
doc: Compute innovation sequence statistics to verify Kalman filter tuning. A well-tuned KF produces white-noise innovations (zero-mean, uncorrelated).

name: solveDiscreteKalman
doc: Steady-state discrete Kalman filter via Riccati difference equation iteration. Solves: P[k+1] = Ad P Ad' + Qd - K S K'  where K = Ad P Cd' S^{-1}, S = Cd P Cd' + Rd

name: simulateLqg
doc: Simulate LQG closed-loop: continuous plant + Luenberger/Kalman observer + LQR feedback. u[k] = -K_lqr * x̂[k]  (feedback on estimated state, not true state) Compare against full state feedback (FSF): u[k] = -K_lqr * x[k]

name: augmentWithIntegralAction
doc: Augment a state-space model with an integral action on tracking error (y - r). Enables zero steady-state error under constant references/disturbances with state feedback u = -[Kx, Ki] * [x; xi].   Aaug = [A, 0; -C, 0] (n+p × n+p)   Baug = [B; 0] (n+p × m)   Caug = [C, 0] (p × n+p)   n = original sta

name: designIntegralLQR
doc: Design integral-action LQR: augment with integrator, then call solveLqr on augmented system. Returns K split into [Kx, Ki]: u = -Kx·x - Ki·xi

name: checkPoleRegion
doc: Check whether a set of eigenvalues satisfies a pole region constraint.   disc:   { alpha, radius }     → |s + alpha| < radius   sector: { zetaMin }           → ζ = -Re(s)/|s| ≥ zetaMin   strip:  { sigmaMin, sigmaMax } → sigmaMin < Re(s) < sigmaMax (both negative)

name: solveHinfFilter
doc: H∞ filter — worst-case optimal state estimation. Solves the modified CARE: A·P + P·A^T - P·S·P + Qw = 0 where S = C^T·Rv⁻¹·C - γ⁻²·I. Uses the LQR duality: this CARE is equivalent to the LQR CARE for the dual system (A^T, L_chol, Qw, I) where L_chol·L_chol^T = S. Requires γ > γ* (feasibility). If γ

name: lqrWithPoleRegion
doc: Find LQR weights that keep closed-loop poles inside a region via iterative Q scaling. Starts from given Q, R and scales Q until all CL poles satisfy the region, up to maxIter.

name: identifyARX
doc: Fit an ARX model.   a: number[],     // [1, a_1, ..., a_na] (high-order first)   b: number[],     // [0,..0, b_1, ..., b_nb] (with leading zeros for nk)   tf: DiscreteTransferFunction,   yhat: number[],  // predicted output (one-step-ahead)   residual: number[],   fitPercent: number,   mse: number,

name: identifyARMAX
doc: ARMAX model identification via iterative pseudo-linear regression. A(q)y = B(q)u + C(q)e, where C(q) = 1 + c₁z⁻¹ + ... + cₙcz⁻ⁿc   a[i]: coefficients of A (i=1..na), b[j]: coefficients of B (j=0..nb-1), c[k]: C coefficients   tf: estimated discrete TransferFunction B(z⁻¹)/A(z⁻¹) (noise model separat

name: identifyOE
doc: Identify Output Error (OE) model using Steiglitz-McBride iteration. Model: y[k] = (B(z^-1) / F(z^-1)) u[k] + e[k]

name: identifyBJ
doc: Identify a Box-Jenkins model: y = B(q)/F(q)·u + C(q)/D(q)·e Uses alternating optimisation (extended Steiglitz-McBride for B/F; pseudo-linear ARMA regression for C/D). Degenerates correctly:   nc=nd=0 → OE;   nf=nd=0 → ARMAX-like (no AR on noise)

name: residualWhitenessTest
doc: Residual whiteness test (autocorrelation + Ljung-Box). A well-fitted model leaves white (uncorrelated) residuals. `passed` is the formal Ljung-Box test at the 5 % significance level:   Q = N(N+2)·Σ r_k²/(N−k) < χ²(nlags, 0.95) The per-lag ±1.96/√N band is provided for visual inspection only.   autoc

name: crossCorrelationTest
doc: Cross-correlation test between residuals and input. For a correctly identified model, residuals e[k] must be uncorrelated with all past inputs u[k-j], j ≥ 1.  The `passed` criterion is the one-sided portmanteau test on negative lags (past inputs):   Q_cc = N · Σ_{j=1}^{nlags} r_{eu}(−j)² < χ²(nlags,

name: computeParameterCovariance
doc: Compute parameter covariance from a normal-equations regression. cov(θ) = σ² · (ΦᵀΦ)⁻¹

name: exportModelUncertainty
doc: Export model uncertainty as a multiplicative frequency-domain bound, compatible with Phase 18 robust validation input format. Uses Monte Carlo sampling from N(θ̂, cov(θ̂)) to compute ±2σ magnitude bounds at each frequency, then reports the maximum relative deviation as a scalar gain variation and wo

name: autoARXOrder
doc: Convenience: try several (na, nb) combinations and pick the one with lowest AIC.

name: autoModelOrder
doc: Multi-structure model order selection with AIC, AICc, BIC and optional cross-validation. Tries every combination of model structure and order, ranks candidates by the chosen information criterion, and optionally evaluates prediction fit on a hold-out validation set.   best: object,   candidates: Arr

name: identifyMISOARX
doc: MISO ARX identification — Multiple-Input Single-Output system. Model: A(q)y[k] = B₁(q)u₁[k] + B₂(q)u₂[k] + … + Bₙᵤ(q)uₙᵤ[k] where the AR part is shared and each input has its own B polynomial and optional individual delay nk_i. The regression matrix extends the SISO ARX Φ by concatenating the delaye

name: estimateFRF
doc: Estimate the Frequency Response Function (FRF) from input u and output y using Welch's averaged cross-spectrum method (H1 estimator):   H1(ω) = Syu(ω) / Suu(ω) where Syu is the cross-spectrum and Suu is the auto-spectrum of u, both averaged over overlapping Hann-windowed segments.   omega:     numbe

name: fitTFfromFRF
doc: Fit a rational transfer function G(jω) = B(jω)/A(jω) to complex FRF data using Levy's iterative weighted complex curve-fitting method. Model:  G(jω) = (b₀ + b₁(jω) + … + bₙ_b(jω)^nb)                / (1 + a₁(jω) + … + aₙ_a(jω)^na) Algorithm (Levy 1959):   1. Form weighted linear system from Re/Im of

name: generatePRBS
doc: Generate a Pseudo-Random Binary Sequence (PRBS) using a Linear-Feedback Shift Register (LFSR).

name: generateChirp
doc: Generate a swept-frequency cosine signal (Chirp).

name: generateMultiSine
doc: Generate a multi-sine signal (sum of sinusoids).

name: identifySubspace
doc: Subspace Identification (MOESP algorithm) for State-Space models.

name: parseComplexRoot
doc: Parse a complex root string like "-1", "0.5", "-1+2j", "3j". Returns { re, im }.

name: parseRootsString
doc: Parse roots string: comma-separated list of real or complex roots. Complex conjugate pairs: enter ONLY ONE root of the pair (e.g. "-1+2j"). The conjugate is automatically generated. If both z and z* are entered, the duplicate is detected and silently removed.

name: EditorCanvas
doc: canvas.js — Visual Editor Canvas Controller Handles zooming, panning, node/link management, and basic interaction.

name: EditorLink
doc: link.js — Visual Editor Link (Connection)

name: EditorNode
doc: node.js — Visual Editor Block/Node Supports double-click inline editing for TF and Gain labels.

name: Complex
doc: complex.js — Complex number arithmetic for control system computations Provides a lightweight Complex class with all operations needed for transfer function evaluation, root finding, and frequency response.

name: polyval
doc: Evaluate a polynomial (coefficients high→low) at a complex value s. poly = [a_n, a_{n-1}, ..., a_1, a_0] result = a_n * s^n + a_{n-1} * s^{n-1} + ... + a_0

name: SingularMatrixError
doc: Custom error for singular matrices so callers can wrap with context-specific guidance (e.g. RGA, LQR, Lyapunov) instead of leaking a raw "Singular matrix" string to the user.

name: matDet
doc: Determinant (recursive, for small matrices)

name: matInverse
doc: Matrix inverse via Gauss-Jordan (for small matrices)

name: matSolve
doc: Solve AX=B via Gauss-Jordan elimination (small dense systems).

name: matExp
doc: Matrix exponential via scaling-and-squaring + truncated Taylor series. Accurate for small / moderate matrices; sufficient for control workbench use.

name: matRank
doc: Calculate the rank of a matrix using Gaussian elimination

name: rk4
doc: RK4 fixed-step solver.

name: rk45
doc: RK45 (Dormand-Prince) adaptive step solver.

name: interpolateUniform
doc: Linear interpolation of ODE output at uniform time points.

name: solveEqualityQP
doc: Solve  min 0.5 xᵀHx + fᵀx  s.t.  Aeq x = beq   (Aeq optional). KKT system:  [H  Aeqᵀ; Aeq  0] [x; λ] = [−f; beq].

name: solveQP
doc: Solve a convex QP:   minimize    0.5 xᵀ H x + fᵀ x   subject to  A x ≤ b , Aeq x = beq , lb ≤ x ≤ ub   x: number[], fval: number, iterations: number, converged: boolean,   lambda: number[], nu: number[], method: string }}

name: solveBoxQP
doc: Solve box-constrained QP:  min 0.5 xᵀHx + fᵀx  s.t. lb ≤ x ≤ ub. Thin wrapper over solveQP for the most common control case.

name: solveLP
doc: Solve a linear program:   minimize    cᵀ x   subject to  A x ≤ b , Aeq x = beq , lb ≤ x ≤ ub Implemented as a strictly-convex QP with a vanishing quadratic term (H = reg·I). For LPs with a unique optimal vertex the solution converges to the LP optimum as reg→0; for degenerate/multiple-optima LPs it

name: symmetricEig
doc: Cyclic Jacobi eigendecomposition of a symmetric matrix.          and vectors[:,k] is the k-th eigenvector (columns of V).

name: solveSDP
doc: Solve a standard-form SDP / LMI:   minimize    cᵀ x   subject to  F(x) = F0 + Σ x_i F_i  ⪰  0      (linear matrix inequality) Method: ADMM splitting with F(x) − S = 0, S ⪰ 0.   • x-update: least-squares solve using the Gram matrix ⟨F_i, F_j⟩   • S-update: projection onto the PSD cone (eigenvalue cla

name: solveLMIFeasibility
doc: Convenience: pure LMI feasibility — find x s.t. F0 + Σ x_i F_i ⪰ 0.

name: polyderiv
doc: Derivative of polynomial in high-degree-first form.

name: rootsToRealPoly
doc: Convert roots to real polynomial coefficients. Complex roots must form conjugate pairs. Pair matching uses a best-match strategy with relative tolerance to handle numerical drift from root finders (Durand-Kerner can drift ~1e-5 for ill-conditioned / repeated roots).

name: polyroots
doc: Find roots via companion matrix eigenvalues

name: polydiv
doc: Polynomial long division: a(s) / b(s) = q(s) remainder r(s). Coefficients [high → low].

name: realSchur
doc: Compute the real Schur decomposition A = Q T Q' where T is quasi-upper triangular (real Schur form) and Q is orthogonal.

name: hamiltonianStableSubspace
doc: Extract the n-dimensional stable invariant subspace of the Hamiltonian H (2n×2n) via the matrix sign function (Newton iteration). The matrix sign function satisfies sign(H) = I on the unstable subspace and sign(H) = -I on the stable subspace, so Ps = (I − sign(H)) / 2 is the exact spectral projector

name: mulberry32
doc: Mulberry32 — small, fast 32-bit hash-based PRNG with good statistical properties. Produces a function returning floats in [0, 1).

name: setSeed
doc: Set a deterministic seed. Pass `null` to revert to Math.random.

name: rand
doc: Uniform float in [0, 1).

name: randn
doc: Normal-distributed sample (Box-Muller).

name: resetSeed
doc: Reset the generator to its initial seed (so simulations restart reproducibly).

name: computeSVD
doc: Compute the SVD of a matrix A (m x n) where m >= n.

name: ariaAttrs
doc: Build an ARIA attribute string from a plain object. Keys are auto-prefixed with "aria-" if not already.

name: hiddenLabel
doc: Emit a hidden <span> label that can be associated via aria-labelledby / aria-describedby.

name: formField
doc: Wrap content with an accessible label for a form field.

name: liveRegion
doc: Build an aria-live region for dynamic announcements.

name: skipLink
doc: "Skip to main content" accessibility link (visually hidden until focused).

name: iconButton
doc: Accessible icon-only button (requires ariaLabel).

name: focusRingCSS
doc: CSS rules for keyboard focus visibility (WCAG 2.4.7 + 2.4.11).

name: relativeLuminance
doc: Relative luminance of a CSS hex colour (WCAG 2.1 definition).

name: contrastRatio
doc: WCAG contrast ratio between two hex colours.

name: meetsWCAG
doc: Check if a colour pair meets WCAG contrast thresholds.

name: describeKey
doc: Generate a human-readable keyboard shortcut label.

name: srOnlyCSS
doc: Standard .cs-sr-only CSS rule (visually hidden but accessible).

name: createZoomState
doc: Create a zoom-state manager for a 2-D chart.   view: { xMin,xMax,yMin,yMax },   zoom: (factor, cx, cy) => void,   pan:  (dx, dy) => void,   reset: () => void,   setView: (v) => void,   getView: () => { xMin,xMax,yMin,yMax }, }}

name: windowData
doc: Extract data points within a [xMin, xMax] window.

name: downsampleLTTB
doc: Largest-triangle-three-buckets (LTTB) downsampling. Preserves visual shape while reducing point count.

name: buildSVGChart
doc: Generate a minimal SVG line chart skeleton. Data series are rendered as <polyline> elements.

name: toCSV
doc: Format chart series data as a CSV string.

name: toJSON
doc: Format chart data as a JSON object suitable for external tools.

name: formatAxisLabel
doc: Format a number for use as an axis tick label. Chooses between fixed/exponential based on magnitude.

name: esc
doc: HTML-escape a string to prevent XSS injection.

name: badge
doc: Semantic status badge.

name: button
doc: Accessible button element.

name: table
doc: Accessible data table with optional caption.

name: alert
doc: Alert / callout panel.

name: panel
doc: Collapsible panel / card.

name: progressBar
doc: Progress bar (0–100).

name: spinner
doc: Spinning loader (aria-busy wrapper).

name: tabs
doc: Accessible tab strip (ARIA tabs pattern).

name: kvList
doc: Definition-list style key-value display.

name: buildCSSVars
doc: Build a CSS string with :root custom properties for the given theme.

name: buildAdaptiveCSS
doc: Build a full CSS variable block that includes both themes via prefers-color-scheme.

name: BREAKPOINTS
doc: Breakpoint definitions (mobile-first, pixel widths).

name: mediaQuery
doc: Build a CSS `@media` query string for a given breakpoint.

name: responsiveGrid
doc: Build a complete responsive layout CSS block for a grid container.

name: detectTheme
doc: Detect preferred theme. In Node.js, always returns 'dark'. In browser, reads data-theme attribute or prefers-color-scheme.

name: applyTheme
doc: Apply a theme by setting [data-theme] on the <html> element. (Browser-only; no-op in Node.)

name: toggleTheme
doc: Toggle between light and dark themes.

name: renderWorkflowStep
doc: Render one workflow step as an HTML card.

name: renderWizardPanel
doc: Render the complete design wizard output as an HTML panel.   `result` is the raw designWizard() output for programmatic use.

name: buildWizardForm
doc: Build an HTML form that collects design spec inputs for the wizard. Does NOT include <form> submit logic — that's the app shell's responsibility.

name: toMatlabScript
doc: Generate a MATLAB script reproducing the current plant + controller + analyses.   plant: {num:number[], den:number[]}|null,   controller: {Kp:number, Ki:number, Kd:number, N:number}|null,   delay: {T:number, order:number}|null,   domain: 's'|'z',   Ts: number|null,   responseType: string,   closedLo

name: toPythonScript
doc: Generate a Python script using python-control (`pip install control`).

name: downloadScript
doc: Trigger a browser download of the generated script.

name: fmtNum
doc: format.js — Number and polynomial formatting utilities

name: parsePolyString
doc: Parse polynomial from string input. Extremely robust: extracts all numbers regardless of brackets/parentheses.

name: polyToLatex
doc: Format a polynomial coefficient array (high-order first) into a LaTeX string. Examples:   [1] → "1"   [1, 0] → "s"   [1, 3, 2] → "s^2 + 3s + 2"   [-1, 2] → "-s + 2"

name: tfToLatex
doc: Build a LaTeX rational TF \frac{num}{den}.

name: renderLatex
doc: Render a LaTeX string into a DOM element using KaTeX (loaded async via CDN). Falls back to plain text if KaTeX is unavailable.

name: renderTfLatex
doc: Convenience: render a TF directly.

name: pidToLatex
doc: PID transfer function as LaTeX: K_p + K_i/s + K_d s
